rationale: Both correctly land on 'duplicate of #4808,' matching the thread resolution, but Y also cites #4809 as the tracking issue exactly as the ground-truth comment does, while X never mentions #4809 at all. X asserts confidently throughout that #4808 is 'merged' and the fix is 'already fixed on main,' only walking it back with a bolted-on disclaimer at the end — an internally inconsistent, less trustworthy pattern. Y instead flags the merge status and a plausible test-coverage gap as open questions to confirm rather than facts, which is more appropriate given neither candidate actually verified state via tools, and this framing makes Y more complete (it addresses whether the closure is actually safe, not just whether the diagnosis is right).
